rejection_reasons: General weakness is broader than focal or limb weakness and remains materially under-specified.
No test or action is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: No legacy item in gp-general-weakness remains source-supported after conservative correction; item-specific population applicability was not independently established without new research.
setting_applicability: No legacy item in gp-general-weakness remains source-supported after conservative correction; transfer from the recorded source setting to the exact workflow item was not independently established.
UAE_applicability: insufficient_evidence for every removed gp-general-weakness legacy-item mapping; UAE applicability requires independent item-level review before any mapping is reinstated.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult weakness section supports documenting sudden, progressive, fluctuating, unilateral, symmetrical, sensory-associated, and back-pain-associated weakness plus functional impact without assigning a neurological diagnosis. The exact section supports clinician-assessed focal neurology, gait, imbalance, bowel or bladder, systemic concern, existing results, referral status, and follow-up without inferring examination or urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports sudden, progressive, fluctuating, unilateral, symmetrical, sensory-associated, and back-pain-associated weakness, urgent referral, and clinician-led imaging where specified.

unresolved_source_gaps: Weakness versus fatigue, distribution, onset, observations, neurological examination, gait, systemic symptoms, medicines, cause, diagnosis, tests, treatment, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
